method_atom: A strong-to-weak-coupling duality that maps a strongly-interacting many-body system at finite temperature and finite charge density onto a tractable higher-dimensional geometric problem with a horizon representing dissipation. Linear-response transport coefficients — a full conductivity tensor including a transverse (Hall) component — are extracted by turning on weak external driving fields conjugate to a conserved charge and reading off the induced steady-state current; in the heavy-carrier limit the dissipative drag on charge carriers is obtained from the same geometric configuration.
object_atom: Massive N=2 supersymmetric hypermultiplet flavor fields carrying baryon charge at finite density, propagating through an N=4 supersymmetric SU(Nc) Yang-Mills plasma in the large-Nc, large 't Hooft coupling limit, whose gravitational dual is a probe D7-brane with a worldvolume gauge field in an AdS-Schwarzschild background.
brief_reason: The duality-based extraction of a conductivity tensor and drag force via external fields and a horizon carries across as transferable machinery, while the specific flavored gauge-theory plasma and its probe-D7-brane dual is the string-theory/high-energy-physics-specific object.
